pi_items.metadata_value: anti_pattern4_max_partial=55 (boiler=20, ports_unlinked=15, new_file=20), threshold=70 requires proper port nesting
pi_items.verified_value: threshold=70 does not require proper port nesting because new_file=20 + boiler=20 + ports_unlinked=30 equals 70

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for blenderbim_env mep_equipment_port_definition task. The data uses the real FZK-Haus IFC test model (not synthetic). Verified all output file paths, specification locations, and required port/boiler counts directly against the task scripts and verifier. Discovered a critical contradiction in the scoring rubric note: while the metadata claims that a passing score of 70 strictly requires proper port nesting and that unlinked ports max out at 55, the verifier.py logic actually awards 20 pts (new file) + 20 pts (1 boiler) + 30 pts (2 unlinked ports) = 70 pts. This completely invalidates the metadata claim and allows agents to pass the task without successfully linking any ports.
